title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed the lawsuit of Babson student Any Lucia Lopez Belloza, mistakenly deported to Honduras.
Lopez Belloza was deported despite a court order, as she declined a flight that nullified court jurisdiction.
Her lawyer argued ICE uses controversial tactics to obscure detainees' locations; an appeal was filed to keep the case in Boston.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson calls Derrick White a 'top five player' in the league based on analytics.
Derrick White averages 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists despite Jayson Tatum's absence for most of the season.
White's versatile defense and statistical contributions make him a crucial player on the Boston Celtics roster.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics win 109-98 against the Cleveland Cavaliers, with Jaylen Brown scoring 23 points and Jayson Tatum 20.
Jayson Tatum shows promising form in recovery, scoring 20 points in his second game post-injury but still not fully healthy.
Derrick White praised by coaches, considered one of NBA's best by Kenny Atkinson, contributing significantly on both ends.

title: Former Patriots WR cautions against signing Alec Pierce to a hefty contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are considering Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce as a free-agent target to replace Stefon Diggs.
Alec Pierce has led the NFL in yards per catch, but his new contract may exceed $27 million per year.
Former Patriots WR Chris Hogan warns against paying over $30 million for Pierce, citing limitations in route versatility.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown Police Officer Brittany Dougal, 29, died by suicide after battling mental illness, the department reported.
Dougal graduated from the Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy in December and was recently sworn into the North Kingstown Police Department.
Her family requests donations to the North Kingstown Animal Shelter or Tunnel to Towers Foundation in her memory.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots are considering under-the-radar players like Vikings WR Jalen Nailor and Giants TE Daniel Bellinger for free agency.
Teven Jenkins and Daniel Faalele are potential guard options after the Patriots traded Garrett Bradbury.
Bengals EDGE Joseph Ossai and Lions EDGE Al-Quadin Muhammad are among notable defensive targets for the Patriots.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, a Raiders minority owner, reportedly influenced trade decisions involving edge rusher Maxx Crosby.
The Patriots were reportedly interested in acquiring Maxx Crosby, but he was traded to the Ravens for two first-round picks.
Brady has reportedly been involved in Raiders' football operations, including hiring head coach Klint Kubiak.

title: Rumored Patriots target Alec Pierce reportedly expected to make at least $27M per year, might not hit free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Colts chose not to use franchise tag on Alec Pierce, increasing the possibility of his free agency entry.
Alec Pierce is anticipated to command a contract of at least $27 million annually, with potential Colts re-signing efforts underway.
Patriots are exploring wide receiver options, including rumored interest in Alec Pierce amid their recent release of Stefon Diggs.

title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, 30, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Boston Public Library.
Flynn overcame dyslexia and ADHD, finding support in libraries, which she considers sanctuaries.
She faces a rare vascular condition, having undergone four leg surgeries, making marathon training challenging.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, will run her 13th Boston Marathon in 2026 to support the Ellie Fund.
Comander is dedicating her run to her patient, Christina, who is battling advanced breast cancer and has qualified for Boston 12 times.
The Ellie Fund provides support services like transportation, meals, and childcare to breast cancer patients to ease daily burdens.
